seoTitle: How to Create a Form in Freshdesk
metaDescription: Learn how to build a custom ticket form in Freshdesk to collect the exact information your support team needs from customers.
intro: Custom ticket forms help you gather the right details upfront so agents spend less time chasing missing information. This guide walks through creating a new form in Freshdesk.

steps.heading: Open the ticket fields or forms section
steps.body: In the admin panel, find the section for managing ticket fields and forms.

steps.heading: Create a new form
steps.body: Start a new form and give it a clear name that reflects its purpose, such as a specific request type.

steps.heading: Add and arrange fields
steps.body: Add the fields you need, such as text boxes, dropdowns, or checkboxes, and arrange them in a logical order.

steps.heading: Publish the form
steps.body: Save and publish the form so it becomes available for customers or agents to use when creating tickets.

faq.question: Can I have multiple forms in Freshdesk?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create multiple forms for different ticket types or departments.

faq.question: Can I make fields required on a form?
faq.answer: Yes, you can mark specific fields as mandatory so tickets can't be submitted without them.

faq.question: Do custom forms work on the customer portal?
faq.answer: Yes, custom forms can be made available on your customer-facing portal as well as internally.

faq.question: Can I edit a form after publishing it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can go back and adjust fields or layout on a published form at any time.
